speaker: KENT
text: Why the King of France is so suddenly gone

speaker: KENT
text: back know you no reason?

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Something he left imperfect in the state,

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: which since his coming forth is thought of, which

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: imports to the kingdom so much fear and danger

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: that his personal return was most required and

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: necessary.

speaker: KENT
text: Who hath he left behind him general?

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: The Marshal of France, Monsieur La Far.

speaker: KENT
text: Did your letters pierce the Queen to any demonstration

speaker: KENT
text: of grief?

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Ay, sir, she took them, read them in my presence,

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: And now and then an ample tear trilled down

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Her delicate cheek. It seemed she was a queen

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Over her passion, who, most rebel-like,

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Fought to be king o’er her.

speaker: KENT
text: O, then it moved her.

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Not to a rage. Patience and sorrow strove

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Who should express her goodliest. You have seen

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Sunshine and rain at once; her smiles and tears

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Were like a better way. Those happy smilets

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: That played on her ripe lip seemed not to know

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: What guests were in her eyes, which parted thence

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: As pearls from diamonds dropped. In brief,

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Sorrow would be a rarity most beloved

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: If all could so become it.

speaker: KENT
text: Made she no verbal question?

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Faith, once or twice she heaved the name of “father”

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Pantingly forth, as if it pressed her heart;

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Cried “Sisters, sisters, shame of ladies, sisters!

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Kent, father, sisters! What, i’ th’ storm, i’ th’ night?

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Let pity not be believed!” There she shook

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: The holy water from her heavenly eyes,

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: And clamor moistened. Then away she started,

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: To deal with grief alone.

speaker: KENT
text: It is the stars.

speaker: KENT
text: The stars above us govern our conditions,

speaker: KENT
text: Else one self mate and make could not beget

speaker: KENT
text: Such different issues. You spoke not with her since?

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: No.

speaker: KENT
text: Was this before the King returned?

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: No, since.

speaker: KENT
text: Well, sir, the poor distressèd Lear’s i’ th’ town,

speaker: KENT
text: Who sometime in his better tune remembers

speaker: KENT
text: What we are come about, and by no means

speaker: KENT
text: Will yield to see his daughter.

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Why, good sir?

speaker: KENT
text: A sovereign shame so elbows him—his own unkindness,

speaker: KENT
text: That stripped her from his benediction, turned her

speaker: KENT
text: To foreign casualties, gave her dear rights

speaker: KENT
text: To his dog-hearted daughters—these things sting

speaker: KENT
text: His mind so venomously that burning shame

speaker: KENT
text: Detains him from Cordelia.

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Alack, poor gentleman!

speaker: KENT
text: Of Albany’s and Cornwall’s powers you heard not?

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: ’Tis so. They are afoot.

speaker: KENT
text: Well, sir, I’ll bring you to our master Lear

speaker: KENT
text: And leave you to attend him. Some dear cause

speaker: KENT
text: Will in concealment wrap me up awhile.

speaker: KENT
text: When I am known aright, you shall not grieve

speaker: KENT
text: Lending me this acquaintance. I pray you, go

speaker: KENT
text: Along with me.
